key_insights.insight: Science is an infinite game, offering many smaller, finite competitions along the way.
key_insights.quote: And what we do in science, science is an infinite game, right? You can't win science. But along the way, there's many, many finite games.

key_insights.insight: A good scientist should be open to substantiating or refuting any narrative, even religious ones, with data.
key_insights.quote: What if we could substantiate that narrative? What if we could refute it? A good scientist has to be open to both.

key_insights.insight: The universe's expansion was discovered by observing that galaxies are moving away from each other, suggesting a singularity in the past.
key_insights.quote: Hubble observed that every single galaxy that he could see is moving away from the Milky Way galaxy... He said either we didn't put on our cosmic deodorant and no one wants to be around us, or the universe is getting bigger.

key_insights.insight: Our bodies are literally made of star stuff, elements created in supernovas.
key_insights.quote: So literally, as Carl Sagan said, we are star stuff.

key_insights.insight: If a simulation of reality becomes indistinguishable from reality, a simulator must exist, potentially leading to the concept of a creator.
key_insights.quote: So eventually you get to a point where if you could simulate everything, then you would have to ask, there must be some simulator.

key_insights.insight: Practice has value even in the absence of belief, as it can change you and open you up to experiences.
key_insights.quote: There's a value in practicing even if you don't believe. Just like I say sometimes, even if you got divorced, you should still get married. Because it changes you, and it opens you up to the full panoply of human experience

key_insights.insight: The most compelling evidence of God might not be proof, but rather the human capacity for creation, especially the ability of women to be vessels for life.
key_insights.quote: It's almost impossible for a man to comprehend what it's like to have the ability to be a vessel for life's creation. I think that's part of the evidence for it.

key_insights.insight: We might be alone in the universe, there are a high amount of circumstances that have allowed earth to foster technological life.
key_insights.quote: I think the probability is is extremely small, which is why I saidI think the probability is low that we are that there are other life forms are another way to say itI think it's very high that we are alone

actionable_advice.advice: Express gratitude for the things you have.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reflect daily and verbalize what you are thankful for to amplify gratitude.

actionable_advice.advice: Even without certainty, engage with philosophical or religious practices, in particular gratitude practices.
actionable_advice.how_to: Say a blessing before and after meals, acknowledge something good

memorable_stories: The story of Keating's team's near-Nobel Prize discovery at the South Pole being retracted.
The tale of Barry Barish, a Nobel laureate, experiencing imposter syndrome.
contrarian_claims: Scientists may want to contemplate the possiblity of God.
Probability is not determined by possibility.
Religious people are too comfortable saying that everything is described by God.
The probability is very high that we are alone in the universe.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Brian Keating: I’m Spending $200 Million To Explore Why We Exist! How God Fits Into Science Finally Explained, Is God Real?
